items.headline: SJC Terminal B South Concourse Improvements moves to proposed
items.summary: SJC Terminal B South Concourse Improvements in San Jose has moved from Under Construction to Proposed. The civic project is Terminal B south concourse expansion.

items.headline: Nine new homes proposed for Old Trace Road subdivision
items.summary: The city is considering a plan to subdivide a vacant 1-acre lot into nine single-family homes plus seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This would add 16 new residential units to a previously undeveloped site in the Residential Estate zone.

items.headline: Supermicro invests $2.5M in equipment at Rock Avenue facility
items.summary: Tech company Supermicro is installing new equipment at its 988 Rock Avenue location in a $2.5 million project. This signals continued investment and expansion by a major San José employer in the region.

items.headline: Summerhill redevelopment at El Camino Real moves forward
items.summary: Tree replacement permits issued for the Palo Alto Greens remainder parcel as part of the larger Summerhill redevelopment project at 4345 El Camino Real. This signals progress on a significant residential/mixed-use development that will reshape a major corridor.

items.headline: City approves lease revenue bonds for new Public Safety Building
items.summary: Mountain View's City Council approved financing for a new Public Safety Building project through lease revenue bonds. This represents a major capital investment in city infrastructure that will affect emergency services and public safety operations.

items.headline: City Council to review Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on April 28 to review a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan. This plan could shape how the city manages trees, green space, and environmental protection in coming years.

items.headline: Major mixed-use project proposed: 67 homes and retail space
items.summary: Sunnyvale is considering a 4.22-acre development with 55 townhomes, 12 apartment flats, and over 18,500 square feet of retail space, including a new two-story retail building alongside the existing Speedy's Tacos. The project would be subdivided into 17 lots and represents significant residential and commercial growth in the city.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council considered revising business license tax rates through a municipal code amendment. This annual update affects local businesses operating in Campbell and could impact business costs or city revenue.
